semanticCore: Keeping distance, moving away, remote, turning aside, or separate
quranicRelevance: Turning aside in pride (Na'a - 'wa na'a bi-janibihi'), and keeping others away while staying away ('yan'awna').

keyDerivations.meaning: To move away, keep distance, turn aside, withdraw, or stay remote

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَنَأَىٰ بِجَانِبِهِ (17:83): And he turns aside and moves away/withdraws in pride (the psychology of the ungrateful wealthy).
وَهُمْ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ وَيَنْأَوْنَ عَنْهُ (6:26): And they prevent others from it and they withdraw/stay away from it.
summaryEn: The root represents distancing (Na'ee), describing the prideful withdrawal of ungrateful people and leaders who block others from faith while staying distant themselves.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر التباعد المكاني والمعنوي والترفع كبراً (نأى)، واصفاً إعراض الإنسان البطر بنعمة ربه، وصد مشركي مكة عن القرآن وتنائيهم عنه.
rawText: أَنْآهُ 4 : see آنَاهُ in art. انى. اِسْتَنْأَى 10 : i. q. اِسْتَنَاءَ q. v. in art. نوأ. نَأَى بِهِ 1 [He, or it, removed him; put or placed him at a distance, away, or far away.] (S, art. طرح.) === نَأَى He shrank from a thing: see an ex. in a verse cited voce دَفٌّ. نُؤْىٌ A trench dug round a tent, (S, K,) or a barrier [raised] around it, (T, IB, TA,) to prevent the rain-water from entering it, (S,) or keep off a torrent. (K.)
